annotations.summary: A curated awesome-list of libraries, packages, and resources for quantitative finance, covering pricing, technical indicators, backtesting, portfolio optimization, and market data across Python, R, Julia, and other languages.

Scrapy is a cross-platform web scraping framework for Python 3.10+ that extracts structured data from websites. Maintained by Zyte and community contributors, it is installed with pip and documented for building crawlers and scrapers.

Anime.js is a lightweight, multipurpose JavaScript animation library with a simple, powerful API for animating CSS properties, SVG attributes, DOM attributes, and JavaScript objects, including staggered timing, custom easing, and looped or alternating playback for web interfaces.

A community-curated, categorized list of free public APIs spanning domains such as animals, anime, security, finance, books, and business, used as a discovery reference for developers looking for third-party APIs to integrate.

YOURLS is a self-hosted, open-source URL shortener written in PHP, giving operators full control over their own shortened links, detailed click statistics, and an extensible plugin system on their own server.

FreshRSS is a lightweight, self-hostable RSS and Atom feed aggregator written in PHP, supporting multi-user access, web scraping for sites without feeds, WebSub push updates, and a Google Reader-compatible API for mobile clients.

Crawlee is a TypeScript web scraping and browser automation library for Node.js used to build reliable crawlers. It offers a single interface for HTTP and headless browser crawling with Playwright and Puppeteer, persistent request queues, integrated proxy rotation, and automatic scaling.

Creative Tim UI is a React component library built on top of shadcn/ui for building modern web applications faster. It provides pre-built, customizable components and blocks for Next.js projects, installed through a CLI or the shadcn registry, and configured with Tailwind CSS.

Botpress is an open-source platform for building chatbots and LLM-powered assistants integrated with OpenAI. This repository holds public integrations for the Botpress Hub, developer tools including a CLI, SDK, and API client, and example bots built as code.

Nakama is a scalable open-source game backend server providing user accounts, storage, social features, realtime and turn-based multiplayer, matchmaking, chat, leaderboards, and tournaments. It runs on CockroachDB or Postgres, deploys via Docker or binaries, and is extensible with Lua, TypeScript, or Go.

Community-driven collection of interactive roadmaps, guides, and educational resources that help developers plan learning paths across frontend, backend, DevOps, and other career tracks. Built in TypeScript and organized around clickable topic nodes.

Python library for downloading historical and live market data from Yahoo! Finance, returning results as pandas data structures. Commonly used to fetch stock, financial, and other market data for analysis.
A repository that stores system-administration and management-related scripts, primarily in Python, gathered together so they can be conveniently retrieved and reused.
Application for displaying and controlling an Android device from a computer, supporting screen mirroring and recording. Written in C and built on components such as FFmpeg and SDL2.

A curated list that tracks current concerning and harmful uses of artificial intelligence, covering areas such as discrimination, disinformation, and surveillance, with the goal of raising awareness of AI misuse in society.

A community-compiled collection of well-written, step-by-step guides for recreating popular technologies from scratch, covering topics such as databases, operating systems, shells, Git, emulators, programming languages, and neural networks. It is organized as a categorized list to help developers learn by building.

The Jetpack monorepo holding source code for the Jetpack WordPress plugins along with shared Composer and JavaScript packages used by them. Jetpack offers security, performance, marketing, and design tools for WordPress sites, and the repository documents build steps and a Docker-based local development workflow.

A generic Android deobfuscator that virtually executes an app to understand its behavior, then optimizes the code so it behaves identically but is easier for a human to read. It includes smalivm, a Dalvik virtual machine sandbox, and applies optimizations such as constant propagation, dead code removal, and string decryption.

A free and open-source media system that lets you manage and stream your own media collection from a dedicated server to end-user apps. Jellyfin is a cross-platform .NET alternative to the proprietary Emby and Plex, providing a server backend and API with no premium licenses, hidden features, or strings attached.

A command-line OSINT tool that hunts down social media accounts by a given username across hundreds of social networks. It can be installed through package managers such as pipx and dnf or run via Docker, helping researchers gather publicly available account information from a single query.

Code and pretrained models from OpenAI accompanying the paper Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners. This archived, as-is repository serves as a Python starting point for researchers and engineers to experiment with the GPT-2 language model.

A Python library and command-line tool that embeds and extracts blind, invisible image watermarks using a DWT-DCT-SVD algorithm. The watermark can be recovered without the original image and survives attacks such as rotation, cropping, masking, resizing, and noise.

An introductory Python training course from J.P. Morgan for business analysts and traders, delivered as Jupyter notebooks. It is a motivational demonstration of numerical computing and data visualization for people without a formal programming background, rather than a full computer science curriculum.

Google's publicly shared engineering practices documentation, capturing collective best practices developed over time. It currently centers on the Code Review Guidelines, comprising a Code Reviewer's Guide and a Change Author's Guide, and is offered so other organizations can benefit from the knowledge.

JumpServer is an open-source Privileged Access Management platform, or bastion host, built with Django. It gives DevOps and IT teams on-demand, secure browser-based access to SSH, RDP, Kubernetes, database, and RemoteApp endpoints, with auditing and access control provided through multiple connector components.

A Python library that wraps the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) and ALFRED web services. It returns economic time series as pandas Series or DataFrame objects and provides methods for handling historical data revisions, vintage dates, and full-text series search.

A Go framework for building terminal user interface applications based on The Elm Architecture, structuring programs around a model with Init, Update, and View methods. It supports inline and full-window layouts, a cell-based renderer, keyboard and mouse handling, and clipboard support.

A Python command-line launcher that organizes and installs a large collection of third-party penetration testing tools across categories such as information gathering, wireless attacks, SQL injection, phishing, forensics, and reverse engineering, with keyword search and tag filtering.

An OCR and document parsing toolkit that converts images and PDFs into structured Markdown or JSON output. It offers multilingual text recognition across more than 100 languages plus vision-language models for tables, formulas, and layout, intended to feed data into LLM and RAG pipelines.

Shell scripts and Docker images for running macOS virtual machines inside Docker containers on Linux or Windows using QEMU and KVM. It supports X11 forwarding, SSH and VNC access, USB passthrough, and Kubernetes deployment, and targets CI/CD and macOS security research.

A community-maintained repository collecting links to WWDC events, meetups, keynote watch parties, articles, videos, and tutorials from across the Apple and Swift developer community. Contributors submit pull requests to share events and learning resources, with archives of previous years.

An open source, self-hosted implementation of the Tailscale control server, written in Go. It coordinates WireGuard public keys, assigns client IP addresses, and manages a single private Tailscale network (tailnet), aimed at self-hosters, hobbyists, and small labs.

A React-based toolkit for creating videos programmatically, interactively, or with coding agents. Developers animate content with code, batch-render videos on their own infrastructure, and build video tools or editors, using React code as the source of truth.

A command-line OSINT tool that collects a dossier on a person from a username alone, checking for accounts across thousands of sites and gathering available public information from their pages. It requires no API keys and is used for reconnaissance and investigation.

A modern, simple, privacy-focused web analytics platform and open-source alternative to Google Analytics, Mixpanel, and Amplitude. Self-hosted with Node.js and PostgreSQL, it offers product and web analytics including charts, cohort analysis, and user-journey insights.

AutoScraper is a lightweight Python library for automatic web scraping. Given a URL and sample data, it learns the extraction rules and returns similar elements, then reuses the trained model on new pages to fetch matching or exact content.

LiveKit is an open-source, WebRTC-based platform for adding scalable real-time video, audio, and data to applications. Its Go server runs as a distributed SFU with JWT authentication, client and server SDKs, and single-binary, Docker, or Kubernetes deployment.

GHunt is an offensive Google framework focused on OSINT. It provides CLI modules and a Python library to gather information on email addresses, Gaia IDs, and Drive files, geolocate BSSIDs, and export results as JSON, built with a fully async design.

Depix is a proof-of-concept implementing a technique to recover plaintext from pixelized screenshots, demonstrating that certain pixelization applied to obscure text can be reversed to reveal the original content.

OpenBB Open Data Platform is an open-source toolset that integrates proprietary, licensed, and public financial data into downstream applications. It exposes data to Python, Excel, REST APIs, and MCP servers for AI agents, and ships a CLI plus a FastAPI backend.

A curated list of software services (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, and other offerings) that provide free developer tiers, scoped to services useful to system administrators and DevOps practitioners, assembled from community pull requests and reviews.

Kats is a Python toolkit from Facebook's Infrastructure Data Science team for time series analysis, offering forecasting, anomaly and change-point detection, feature extraction and embedding, and multivariate analysis in one lightweight, extensible framework.

A Node.js library for retrieving real-time market prices, indicator values, and TradingView technical analysis, supporting backtesting, replay mode, custom date ranges, and access to invite-only indicators for building trading bots and alerts.

Cal.diy is the community-driven, open-source edition of Cal.com, a self-hostable scheduling platform for managing bookings and appointments, built with Next.js, Prisma, tRPC, and the T3 stack, and intended for personal, non-production self-hosted use.

FreeMoCap is a free, open-source, hardware- and software-agnostic motion capture system with a Python graphical interface, providing minimal-cost, research-grade markerless motion capture for decentralized scientific research, education, and training.

Ansible is an agentless IT automation platform for configuration management, application deployment, cloud provisioning, ad-hoc task execution, and multi-node orchestration. It uses SSH and a human-readable language to describe infrastructure, managing remote machines in parallel without installing agents on them.

A reference collection of Warren Buffett's annual letters to the shareholders of Berkshire Hathaway spanning 1957 to 2024. The repository gathers these letters as source material on value investing associated with Buffett and his partner Charlie Munger.

Moira is a Chinese astrological chart-casting application (Qi Zheng Si Yu) repackaged here as a portable Windows build made from the original Moira-jre.exe installer with no code changes. It runs on a bundled Java runtime and is launched by clicking Moira.exe, with JRE and class paths configurable in Moira.ini.

Merlion is a Python machine learning library for time series intelligence. It offers an end-to-end framework for forecasting, anomaly detection, and change point detection on univariate and multivariate data, with standardized benchmarking, AutoML hyperparameter tuning and model selection, and post-processing that makes anomaly scores more interpretable.

curl-impersonate is a special build of curl that mimics the TLS and HTTP/2 handshakes of major browsers such as Chrome, Edge, Safari, and Firefox, so requests are not distinguished by fingerprinting. It works as a command-line tool like regular curl or as a drop-in library replacement for libcurl.

A collection of Shadowrocket proxy rule sets for iOS with strong ad-filtering. The rules are automatically generated from sources such as GFWList, EasyList and Peter Lowe's lists, deduplicated, and rebuilt and published every day.

A curated "awesome" list of libraries, packages, and resources for systematic and quantitative trading, spanning crypto, stocks, futures, options, CFDs and FX, with projects selected for domain fit and code quality.

A high-performance, self-hosted photo and video management solution with mobile and web apps for backing up, organizing, and browsing a media library, offered as an open-source alternative to Google Photos.

A fast, feature-rich, cross-platform terminal emulator written in Zig that uses platform-native UI and GPU acceleration. It also provides libghostty, a zero-dependency C and Zig library for embedding terminal functionality.
A comprehensive collection of Chinese-language deep learning study notes in Jupyter notebooks, compiled from popular video courses and covering computer vision, NLP, PyTorch, and large-model agents.
